verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem in diebus illis, exiit edictum a Cæsare Augusto ut describeretur universus orbis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις ἐκείναις ἐξῆλθεν δόγμα παρὰ καίσαρος αὐγούστου ἀπογράφεσθαι πᾶσαν τὴν οἰκουμένην
verses.king_james: And it came to pass in those days, that there went out a decree from Caesar Augustus, that all the world should be taxed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc descriptio prima facta est a præside Syriæ Cyrino:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὕτη ἡ ἀπογραφὴ πρώτη ἐγένετο ἡγεμονεύοντος τῆς συρίας κυρηνίου
verses.king_james: ( *And* this taxing was first made when Cyrenius was governor of Syria.)

verses.latin_vulgate: et ibant omnes ut profiterentur singuli in suam civitatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπορεύοντο πάντες ἀπογράφεσθαι ἕκαστος εἰς τὴν ἰδίαν πόλιν
verses.king_james: And all went to be taxed, every one into his own city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendit autem et Joseph a Galilæa de civitate Nazareth in Judæam, in civitatem David, quæ vocatur Bethlehem: eo quod esset de domo et familia David,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνέβη δὲ καὶ ἰωσὴφ ἀπὸ τῆς γαλιλαίας ἐκ πόλεως ναζαρὲτ εἰς τὴν ἰουδαίαν εἰς πόλιν δαβὶδ, ἥτις καλεῖται βηθλέεμ διὰ τὸ εἶναι αὐτὸν ἐξ οἴκου καὶ πατριᾶς δαβίδ,
verses.king_james: And Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judaea, unto the city of David, which is called Bethlehem; (because he was of the house and lineage of David:)

verses.latin_vulgate: ut profiteretur cum Maria desponsata sibi uxore prægnante.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπογράψασθαι σὺν μαριὰμ τῇ μεμνηστευμένῃ αὐτῷ γυναικὶ, οὔσῃ ἐγκύῳ
verses.king_james: To be taxed with Mary his espoused wife, being great with child.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem, cum essent ibi, impleti sunt dies ut pareret.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ ἐν τῷ εἶναι αὐτοὺς ἐκεῖ ἐπλήσθησαν αἱ ἡμέραι τοῦ τεκεῖν αὐτήν
verses.king_james: And so it was, that, while they were there, the days were accomplished that she should be delivered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et peperit filium suum primogenitum, et pannis eum involvit, et reclinavit eum in præsepio: quia non erat eis locus in diversorio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔτεκεν τὸν υἱὸν αὐτῆς τὸν πρωτότοκον καὶ ἐσπαργάνωσεν αὐτὸν καὶ ἀνέκλινεν αὐτὸν ἐν τῇ φάτνῃ διότι οὐκ ἦν αὐτοῖς τόπος ἐν τῷ καταλύματι
verses.king_james: And she brought forth her firstborn son, and wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger; because there was no room for them in the inn.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et pastores erant in regione eadem vigilantes, et custodientes vigilias noctis super gregem suum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ποιμένες ἦσαν ἐν τῇ χώρᾳ τῇ αὐτῇ ἀγραυλοῦντες καὶ φυλάσσοντες φυλακὰς τῆς νυκτὸς ἐπὶ τὴν ποίμνην αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And there were in the same country shepherds abiding in the field, keeping watch over their flock by night.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce angelus Domini stetit juxta illos, et claritas Dei circumfulsit illos, et timuerunt timore magno.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού, ἄγγελος κυρίου ἐπέστη αὐτοῖς καὶ δόξα κυρίου περιέλαμψεν αὐτούς καὶ ἐφοβήθησαν φόβον μέγαν
verses.king_james: And, lo, the angel of the Lord came upon them, and the glory of the Lord shone round about them: and they were sore afraid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit illis angelus: Nolite timere: ecce enim evangelizo vobis gaudium magnum, quod erit omni populo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἄγγελος μὴ φοβεῖσθε ἰδού, γὰρ εὐαγγελίζομαι ὑμῖν χαρὰν μεγάλην ἥτις ἔσται παντὶ τῷ λαῷ
verses.king_james: And the angel said unto them, Fear not: for, behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy, which shall be to all people.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia natus est vobis hodie Salvator, qui est Christus Dominus, in civitate David.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἐτέχθη ὑμῖν σήμερον σωτὴρ ὅς ἐστιν χριστὸς κύριος ἐν πόλει δαβίδ
verses.king_james: For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Saviour, which is Christ the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hoc vobis signum: invenietis infantem pannis involutum, et positum in præsepio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τοῦτο ὑμῖν τὸ σημεῖον εὑρήσετε βρέφος ἐσπαργανωμένον κείμενον ἐν τῇ φάτνῃ
verses.king_james: And this *shall be* a sign unto you; Ye shall find the babe wrapped in swaddling clothes, lying in a manger.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et subito facta est cum angelo multitudo militiæ cælestis laudantium Deum, et dicentium:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξαίφνης ἐγένετο σὺν τῷ ἀγγέλῳ πλῆθος στρατιᾶς οὐρανίου αἰνούντων τὸν θεὸν καὶ λεγόντων
verses.king_james: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God, and saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: [Gloria in altissimis Deo, et in terra pax hominibus bonæ voluntatis.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δόξα ἐν ὑψίστοις θεῷ καὶ ἐπὶ γῆς εἰρήνη ἐν ἀνθρώποις εὐδοκία
verses.king_james: Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est, ut discesserunt ab eis angeli in cælum: pastores loquebantur ad invicem: Transeamus usque Bethlehem, et videamus hoc verbum, quod factum est, quod Dominus ostendit nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ὡς ἀπῆλθον ἀπ᾽ αὐτῶν εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν οἱ ἄγγελοι καὶ οἱ ἀνθρωποι οἱ ποιμένες εἶπον πρὸς ἀλλήλους διέλθωμεν δὴ ἕως βηθλέεμ καὶ ἴδωμεν τὸ ῥῆμα τοῦτο τὸ γεγονὸς ὃ ὁ κύριος ἐγνώρισεν ἡμῖν
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as the angels were gone away from them into heaven, the shepherds said one to another, Let us now go even unto Bethlehem, and see this thing which is come to pass, which the Lord hath made known unto us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venerunt festinantes: et invenerunt Mariam, et Joseph, et infantem positum in præsepio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦλθον σπεύσαντες καὶ ἀνεῦρον, τήν τε μαριὰμ καὶ τὸν ἰωσὴφ καὶ τὸ βρέφος κείμενον ἐν τῇ φάτνῃ
verses.king_james: And they came with haste, and found Mary, and Joseph, and the babe lying in a manger.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videntes autem cognoverunt de verbo, quod dictum erat illis de puero hoc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδόντες δὲ διεγνώρισαν περὶ τοῦ ῥήματος τοῦ λαληθέντος αὐτοῖς περὶ τοῦ παιδίου τούτου
verses.king_james: And when they had seen *it*, they made known abroad the saying which was told them concerning this child.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omnes qui audierunt, mirati sunt: et de his quæ dicta erant a pastoribus ad ipsos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάντες οἱ ἀκούσαντες ἐθαύμασαν περὶ τῶν λαληθέντων ὑπὸ τῶν ποιμένων πρὸς αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And all they that heard *it* wondered at those things which were told them by the shepherds.

verses.latin_vulgate: Maria autem conservabat omnia verba hæc, conferens in corde suo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ δὲ μαριὰμ πάντα συνετήρει τὰ ῥήματα ταῦτα συμβάλλουσα ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: But Mary kept all these things, and pondered *them* in her heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et reversi sunt pastores glorificantes et laudantes Deum in omnibus quæ audierant et viderant, sicut dictum est ad illos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπέστρεψαν οἱ ποιμένες δοξάζοντες καὶ αἰνοῦντες τὸν θεὸν ἐπὶ πᾶσιν οἷς ἤκουσαν καὶ εἶδον καθὼς ἐλαλήθη πρὸς αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, as it was told unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et postquam consummati sunt dies octo, ut circumcideretur puer, vocatum est nomen ejus Jesus, quod vocatum est ab angelo priusquam in utero conciperetur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτε ἐπλήσθησαν ἡμέραι ὀκτὼ τοῦ περιτεμεῖν τὸ παιδίον, καὶ ἐκλήθη τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ ἰησοῦς τὸ κληθὲν ὑπὸ τοῦ ἀγγέλου πρὸ τοῦ συλληφθῆναι αὐτόν ἐν τῇ κοιλίᾳ
verses.king_james: And when eight days were accomplished for the circumcising of the child, his name was called JESUS, which was so named of the angel before he was conceived in the womb.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et postquam impleti sunt dies purgationis ejus secundum legem Moysi, tulerunt illum in Jerusalem, ut sisterent eum Domino,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτε ἐπλήσθησαν αἱ ἡμέραι τοῦ καθαρισμοῦ αὐτῶν κατὰ τὸν νόμον μωσέως, ἀνήγαγον αὐτὸν εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα παραστῆσαι τῷ κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: And when the days of her purification according to the law of Moses were accomplished, they brought him to Jerusalem, to present *him* to the Lord;

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut scriptum est in lege Domini: Quia omne masculinum adaperiens vulvam, sanctum Domino vocabitur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς γέγραπται ἐν νόμῳ κυρίου ὅτι πᾶν ἄρσεν διανοῖγον μήτραν ἅγιον τῷ κυρίῳ κληθήσεται
verses.king_james: (As it is written in the law of the Lord, Every male that openeth the womb shall be called holy to the Lord;)

verses.latin_vulgate: et ut darent hostiam secundum quod dictum est in lege Domini, par turturum, aut duos pullos columbarum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τοῦ δοῦναι θυσίαν κατὰ τὸ εἰρημένον ἐν νόμῳ κυρίου ζεῦγος τρυγόνων ἢ δύο νεοσσοὺς περιστερῶν
verses.king_james: And to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce homo erat in Jerusalem, cui nomen Simeon, et homo iste justus, et timoratus, exspectans consolationem Israël: et Spiritus Sanctus erat in eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού, ἦν ἄνθρωπος ἐν ἰερουσαλὴμ ᾧ ὄνομα συμεών καὶ ὁ ἄνθρωπος οὗτος δίκαιος καὶ εὐλαβής προσδεχόμενος παράκλησιν τοῦ ἰσραήλ καὶ πνεῦμα ἅγιον ἦν ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And, behold, there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name *was* Simeon; and the same man *was* just and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et responsum acceperat a Spiritu Sancto, non visurum se mortem, nisi prius videret Christum Domini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦν αὐτῷ κεχρηματισμένον ὑπὸ τοῦ πνεύματος τοῦ ἁγίου μὴ ἰδεῖν θάνατον πρὶν ἢ ἴδῃ τὸν χριστὸν κυρίου
verses.king_james: And it was revealed unto him by the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Lord's Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venit in spiritu in templum. Et cum inducerent puerum Jesum parentes ejus, ut facerent secundum consuetudinem legis pro eo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦλθεν ἐν τῷ πνεύματι εἰς τὸ ἱερόν καὶ ἐν τῷ εἰσαγαγεῖν τοὺς γονεῖς τὸ παιδίον ἰησοῦν τοῦ ποιῆσαι αὐτοὺς κατὰ τὸ εἰθισμένον τοῦ νόμου περὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And he came by the Spirit into the temple: and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him after the custom of the law,

verses.latin_vulgate: et ipse accepit eum in ulnas suas: et benedixit Deum, et dixit:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτὸς ἐδέξατο αὐτὸ εἰς τὰς ἀγκάλας αὐτοῦ, καὶ εὐλόγησεν τὸν θεὸν καὶ εἶπεν
verses.king_james: Then took he him up in his arms, and blessed God, and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: [Nunc dimittis servum tuum Domine, secundum verbum tuum in pace:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νῦν ἀπολύεις τὸν δοῦλόν σου δέσποτα κατὰ τὸ ῥῆμά σου ἐν εἰρήνῃ
verses.king_james: Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word:

verses.latin_vulgate: quia viderunt oculi mei salutare tuum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι εἶδον οἱ ὀφθαλμοί μου τὸ σωτήριόν σου
verses.king_james: For mine eyes have seen thy salvation,

verses.latin_vulgate: quod parasti ante faciem omnium populorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃ ἡτοίμασας κατὰ πρόσωπον πάντων τῶν λαῶν
verses.king_james: Which thou hast prepared before the face of all people;

verses.latin_vulgate: lumen ad revelationem gentium, et gloriam plebis tuæ Israël.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φῶς εἰς ἀποκάλυψιν ἐθνῶν καὶ δόξαν λαοῦ σου ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: A light to lighten the Gentiles, and the glory of thy people Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat pater ejus et mater mirantes super his quæ dicebantur de illo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦν ἰωσὴφ καὶ ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ θαυμάζοντες ἐπὶ τοῖς λαλουμένοις περὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And Joseph and his mother marvelled at those things which were spoken of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et benedixit illis Simeon, et dixit ad Mariam matrem ejus: Ecce positus est hic in ruinam et in resurrectionem multorum in Israël, et in signum cui contradicetur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐλόγησεν αὐτοὺς συμεὼν καὶ εἶπεν πρὸς μαριὰμ τὴν μητέρα αὐτοῦ ἰδού, οὗτος κεῖται εἰς πτῶσιν καὶ ἀνάστασιν πολλῶν ἐν τῷ ἰσραὴλ καὶ εἰς σημεῖον ἀντιλεγόμενον
verses.king_james: And Simeon blessed them, and said unto Mary his mother, Behold, this *child* is set for the fall and rising again of many in Israel; and for a sign which shall be spoken against;

verses.latin_vulgate: et tuam ipsius animam pertransibit gladius ut revelentur ex multis cordibus cogitationes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ σοῦ δὲ αὐτῆς τὴν ψυχὴν διελεύσεται ῥομφαία ὅπως ἂν ἀποκαλυφθῶσιν ἐκ πολλῶν καρδιῶν διαλογισμοί
verses.king_james: (Yea, a sword shall pierce through thy own soul also,) that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat Anna prophetissa, filia Phanuel, de tribu Aser: hæc processerat in diebus multis, et vixerat cum viro suo annis septem a virginitate sua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦν ἅννα προφῆτις θυγάτηρ φανουήλ ἐκ φυλῆς ἀσήρ αὕτη προβεβηκυῖα ἐν ἡμέραις πολλαῖς ζήσασα ἔτη μετὰ ἀνδρὸς ἑπτὰ ἀπὸ τῆς παρθενίας αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: And there was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Aser: she was of a great age, and had lived with an husband seven years from her virginity;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hæc vidua usque ad annos octoginta quatuor: quæ non discedebat de templo, jejuniis et obsecrationibus serviens nocte ac die.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτὴ χήρα ὡς ἐτῶν ὀγδοηκοντατεσσάρων, ἣ οὐκ ἀφίστατο ἀπὸ τοῦ ἱεροῦ νηστείαις καὶ δεήσεσιν λατρεύουσα νύκτα καὶ ἡμέραν
verses.king_james: And she *was* a widow of about fourscore and four years, which departed not from the temple, but served *God* with fastings and prayers night and day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hæc, ipsa hora superveniens, confitebatur Domino: et loquebatur de illo omnibus, qui exspectabant redemptionem Israël.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτῇ αὕτη τῇ ὥρᾳ ἐπιστᾶσα ἀνθωμολογεῖτο τῷ κυρίῳ, καὶ ἐλάλει περὶ αὐτοῦ πᾶσιν τοῖς προσδεχομένοις λύτρωσιν ἐν ἰερουσαλήμ
verses.king_james: And she coming in that instant gave thanks likewise unto the Lord, and spake of him to all them that looked for redemption in Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ut perfecerunt omnia secundum legem Domini, reversi sunt in Galilæam in civitatem suam Nazareth.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὡς ἐτέλεσαν ἅπαντα τὰ κατὰ τὸν νόμον κυρίου ὑπέστρεψαν εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν εἰς τὴν πόλιν αὑτῶν ναζαρέτ
verses.king_james: And when they had performed all things according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city Nazareth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Puer autem crescebat, et confortabatur plenus sapientia: et gratia Dei erat in illo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ δὲ παιδίον ηὔξανεν καὶ ἐκραταιοῦτο πνεύματι, πληρούμενον σοφίας καὶ χάρις θεοῦ ἦν ἐπ᾽ αὐτό
verses.king_james: And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit, filled with wisdom: and the grace of God was upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ibant parentes ejus per omnes annos in Jerusalem, in die solemni Paschæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπορεύοντο οἱ γονεῖς αὐτοῦ κατ᾽ ἔτος εἰς ἰερουσαλὴμ τῇ ἑορτῇ τοῦ πάσχα
verses.king_james: Now his parents went to Jerusalem every year at the feast of the passover.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum factus esset annorum duodecim, ascendentibus illis Jerosolymam secundum consuetudinem diei festi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτε ἐγένετο ἐτῶν δώδεκα ἀναβάντων αὐτῶν εἰς ἰεροσόλυμα, κατὰ τὸ ἔθος τῆς ἑορτῆς
verses.king_james: And when he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem after the custom of the feast.

verses.latin_vulgate: consummatisque diebus, cum redirent, remansit puer Jesus in Jerusalem, et non cognoverunt parentes ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τελειωσάντων τὰς ἡμέρας ἐν τῷ ὑποστρέφειν αὐτοὺς ὑπέμεινεν ἰησοῦς ὁ παῖς ἐν ἰερουσαλήμ καὶ οὐκ ἔγνω ἰωσὴφ καὶ ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And when they had fulfilled the days, as they returned, the child Jesus tarried behind in Jerusalem; and Joseph and his mother knew not *of it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Existimantes autem illum esse in comitatu, venerunt iter diei, et requirebant eum inter cognatos et notos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νομίσαντες δὲ αὐτὸν ἐν τῇ συνοδίᾳ εἶναι ἦλθον ἡμέρας ὁδὸν καὶ ἀνεζήτουν αὐτὸν ἐν τοῖς συγγενέσιν καὶ ἐν τοῖς γνωστοῖς
verses.king_james: But they, supposing him to have been in the company, went a day's journey; and they sought him among *their* kinsfolk and acquaintance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non invenientes, regressi sunt in Jerusalem, requirentes eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μὴ εὑρόντες αὐτόν ὑπέστρεψαν εἰς ἰερουσαλὴμ ζητοῦντες αὐτὸν,
verses.king_james: And when they found him not, they turned back again to Jerusalem, seeking him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est, post triduum invenerunt illum in templo sedentem in medio doctorum, audientem illos, et interrogantem eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο μεθ᾽ ἡμέρας τρεῖς εὗρον αὐτὸν ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ καθεζόμενον ἐν μέσῳ τῶν διδασκάλων καὶ ἀκούοντα αὐτῶν καὶ ἐπερωτῶντα αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that after three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the doctors, both hearing them, and asking them questions.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stupebant autem omnes qui eum audiebant, super prudentia et responsis ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξίσταντο δὲ πάντες οἱ ἀκούοντες αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τῇ συνέσει καὶ ταῖς ἀποκρίσεσιν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And all that heard him were astonished at his understanding and answers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et videntes admirati sunt. Et dixit mater ejus ad illum: Fili, quid fecisti nobis sic? ecce pater tuus et ego dolentes quærebamus te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδόντες αὐτὸν ἐξεπλάγησαν καὶ πρὸς αὐτὸν ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ εἶπεν τέκνον τί ἐποίησας ἡμῖν οὕτως ἰδού, ὁ πατήρ σου κἀγὼ ὀδυνώμενοι ἐζητοῦμέν σε
verses.king_james: And when they saw him, they were amazed: and his mother said unto him, Son, why hast thou thus dealt with us? behold, thy father and I have sought thee sorrowing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait ad illos: Quid est quod me quærebatis? nesciebatis quia in his quæ Patris mei sunt, oportet me esse?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς τί ὅτι ἐζητεῖτέ με οὐκ ᾔδειτε ὅτι ἐν τοῖς τοῦ πατρός μου δεῖ εἶναί με
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, How is it that ye sought me? wist ye not that I must be about my Father's business?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ipsi non intellexerunt verbum quod locutus est ad eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτοὶ οὐ συνῆκαν τὸ ῥῆμα ὃ ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And they understood not the saying which he spake unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et descendit cum eis, et venit Nazareth: et erat subditus illis. Et mater ejus conservabat omnia verba hæc in corde suo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ κατέβη μετ᾽ αὐτῶν καὶ ἦλθεν εἰς ναζαρέτ καὶ ἦν ὑποτασσόμενος αὐτοῖς καὶ ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ διετήρει πάντα τὰ ῥήματα ταῦτα ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth, and was subject unto them: but his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Jesus proficiebat sapientia, et ætate, et gratia apud Deum et homines.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰησοῦς προέκοπτεν σοφίᾳ καὶ ἡλικίᾳ καὶ χάριτι παρὰ θεῷ καὶ ἀνθρώποις
verses.king_james: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favour with God and man.
